info.title: Illustrator API - Firefly Services
info.description: Illustrator API allows you to perform various operations with an Adobe Illustrator template. The API processes requests asynchronously.

tags.name: Data Merge & Create Rendition
tags.description: Data merge, create rendition, and shared job status for asynchronous Illustrator API jobs.

tags.name: Custom Scripts
tags.description: Submit and execute custom scripts for Illustrator automation. Supports comprehensive scripting capabilities for document manipulation and processing

post.description: This endpoint allows you to merge variable data from a CSV file with an Adobe Illustrator template. The request is processed asynchronously and the status of the job can be checked using the status URL provided in the response. The output is provided as an array of individual files, one for each row in the CSV data. If some rows succeed and others fail or require rerun, the job completes with status `partially_succeeded` when you poll the status endpoint; that status applies only to Data Merge jobs.

post.description: This endpoint allows you to convert Adobe Illustrator files into various output formats. It supports conversion of .ai documents into multiple web- and print-ready formats. The request is processed asynchronously and the status of the job can be checked using the status URL provided in the response. The output is provided as a single converted file.

get.description:
Retrieve the status of a job by providing the job ID. The job ID can be obtained from the response of other APIs. For all asynchronous jobs, polling this endpoint can return `running`, `succeeded`, or `failed`. The `partially_succeeded` status is returned only for Data Merge jobs when some CSV rows produce outputs while other rows fail or must be rerun after errors (for example, missing fonts). No other job types return the `partially_succeeded` status.
When `status` is `succeeded`, for Data Merge jobs each item in `outputs` includes a `row` field; For Create Rendition jobs, output items contain `destination` and `mediaType` only.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The job ID to retrieve the status of.

post.description: Submits custom script bundles for registration. Returns a URL for posting execution requests for the registered script.

requestBody.description: The custom script bundle, in `multipart/form-data`. The ZIP is required and must describe the script to the CXP app engine (see the `file` part schema).

schema.description: The custom script bundle, in ZIP format. This package describes the script to the app engine.

file.description: The custom script bundle, in ZIP format. Required contents: `main.jsx` (ExtendScript with `main()` entry point) and `manifest.json` (capability metadata).

post.summary: Submit a custom script execution request
post.description: Submits execution requests for custom scripts. Defines input assets and parameters that the custom script will use during execution.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Path segment for the org or scope, as in the `url` returned in the custom script **Submit a Custom Script** response (same role as a script scope identifier in other Custom Scripts products).

parameters.name: capabilityName
parameters.description: The name of the custom script, specified in the custom script `manifest.json` and registration response (same idea as a script name in custom script manifest-based APIs).

requestBody.description: This job request object specifies parameters for the execution of the custom script.

202.description: The job has been accepted for processing.

get.description: Poll execution status for a Custom Scripts job. When the job succeeds, each entry in `outputs` includes a presigned URL to download a file that was written under the output directory you specified in `params` when starting the job

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Job ID returned from execute capability.

AccessToken.description: The Adobe-generated access token, S2S format.

url.description: The URL pointing to the resource. Only the following allow-listed domains can be used: amazonaws.com, windows.net, dropboxusercontent.com, assets.frame.io, storage.googleapis.com.

source.description: The source containing the CSV file with variable data. The media type defaults to `text/csv`.
The source containing the Adobe Illustrator template file.

DataMergeFontFileMediaType.description: The media type of the font file. Supports font/ttf and font/otf.

source.description: The source containing the font file.

autoReduceFontSizeToFit.description: Automatically reduce font size to fit text within text boxes. Defaults to false if not specified.

minFontSize.description: Minimum font size (in points) when `autoReduceFontSizeToFit` is enabled. Must be a positive number.

fallBackFont.description: A case-sensitive PostScript font name (like Roboto-Thin, Oxygen, or SourceSansPro-Bold) to use as a fallback when the fonts are missing in the template file.

fontSettings.description: Settings for font handling during the merge operation.

DataMergeOutputMediaType.description: The desired output media type for the merged documents.

fileName.description: The output file name. May or may not have an extension. It is recommended to provide with extension (e.g., 'result.png', 'output.pdf').

data.description: The data source containing variable data. Must be a CSV file.

fontFiles.description: Optional array of font files to be used in the template. Supports .ttf and .otf font formats.

settings.description: Settings for the data merge operation.

output.description: Output specification for the merge operation with file name and media type.

message.example: Invalid request payload. Check validation_errors for details.

validation_errors.description: An array of various validation errors.

url.description: The URL to the output file.

DataMergeJobApiResponse.title: Succeeded (Data Merge)
DataMergeJobApiResponse.description: Returned when a Data Merge job completes successfully. Each output includes the CSV row index.

outputs.description: Array of output files from the data merge operation, one per CSV row.

JobStatusPollPayload.title: Running
JobStatusPollPayload.description: Returned while the job is still processing. Poll again after the interval indicated by `Retry-After`.

Retry-After.description: Suggested number of seconds to wait before sending another request to this endpoint.

message.example: Missing fonts detected: Chabplan-Rg. Please provide font files.

DataMergeRowRange.description: An inclusive range of CSV row indices.

start.description: The start row index for the range.

end.description: The end row index for the range.

DataMergeNeedsRerunItem.description: A row range that must be rerun after errors are resolved.

rowRange.description: The inclusive range of CSV rows to reprocess.

DataMergePartialSuccessError.description: An error for a specific CSV row when the job completes with partial success.

row.description: The CSV row index where the error occurred.

error_code.description: The error code for this row.

message.description: The error message for this row.
message.example: Missing fonts detected: Chabplan-Rg. Please provide font files.

DataMergeOutputWithRow.description: A merged output file for a specific CSV row.

row.description: The CSV row index for this output.

destination.description: Details of the location where the output is located.

DataMergeJobPartiallySucceededResponse.title: Partially Succeeded
DataMergeJobPartiallySucceededResponse.description: Returned ONLY FOR DATA MERGE jobs when some rows produced outputs while others failed or require rerun. Other job types do not use this status.

outputs.description: Output files produced for successfully merged rows.

errors.description: Per-row errors for rows that did not produce outputs.

needsRerun.description: Row ranges that must be rerun after addressing errors (for example, missing fonts).

url.description: The URL pointing to the Adobe Illustrator file. Only the following allow-listed domains can be used: amazonaws.com, windows.net, dropboxusercontent.com, assets.frame.io, storage.googleapis.com.

CreateRenditionSourceMediaType.description: The media type of the source file.

source.description: The source containing the Adobe Illustrator file.

mediaType.description: The media type of the source file. Optional, defaults to application/illustrator.

CreateRenditionOutputMediaType.description: The desired output media type for the converted file.

documentLevel.description: Document level - 0 for artboard level, 1 for document level

artboardRange.description: Artboard range to export (e.g., '1-3', '1,2,5'). Required when documentLevel is 0.

resolution.description: Resolution in DPI (dots per inch) for the output. Must be an integer between 4 and 2400 for PNG and JPEG formats. Defaults to 72. Not applicable for other formats (PDF, EPS, SVG, or WEBP).

input.description: The input object containing source and media type.

output.description: Output specification for the rendition operation with desired media type.

settings.description: Settings for controlling the rendition process.

url.description: The URL to the output file.

destination.description: Details of the location where the output is located.

CreateRenditionJobApiResponse.title: Succeeded (Create Rendition)
CreateRenditionJobApiResponse.description: Returned when a Create Rendition job completes successfully. Output items do not include a CSV row index.

outputs.description: Array of converted output files (one for each artboard).

CapabilityRegistrationResponse.description: Response after a successful custom script submit. The `url` is the base URL to use for **Submit a custom script execution request** (append path segments or use it as the execute endpoint, depending on the value returned).

url.description: Base URL for posting custom script execution requests.

capability.description: Registered custom script or capability name (from `manifest.json`).

url.description: Presigned URL pointing to the asset.

CustomScriptAssetItem.description: Information about an input asset the job will use (file reference and local destination name in the job workspace).

destination.description:
Local filename for the asset in the job workspace (referenced by `targetDocument` and other params). 
 This provides a path relative to a temporary base directory (working directory) where the resource would be downloaded. This would be used to identify the assets within the parameters. It cannot go up using '..' or '/'. or './' Should be a valid file name.

targetDocument.description: Filename of the Illustrator document to process.

outputFolderPath.description: Output folder path for generated files.

zip.description: Optional. Destination filename of a zipped asset when inputs are packaged as a ZIP.

fontFolderPath.description:
Optional. Relative path to a font folder when required by the script. 
 This provides a path relative to a temporary base directory (working directory) where the resource would be downloaded. This would be used to identify the assets within the parameters. It cannot go up using '..' or '/'.  or './' Should be a valid file name. To use this parameter, the `zip` parameter must also be provided.

CustomScriptExecuteRequest.description: This job request object specifies parameters for the execution of the custom script.

assets.description: Array of **Input assets**. Information about the assets, which will be used to process the job (for example AI documents, font archives, linked or packaged assets, and so on).

CustomScriptExecuteResponse.description: Returned when the execution request is accepted (HTTP 202).

jobId.description: Job identifier. Use with **Retrieve custom script job status** on `GET /v3/status/{jobId}`.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for custom script job status.

CustomScriptJobConversionDetails.description: Details of the conversion performed during execution.

inputFile.description: Name of the input Illustrator file.

destination.description: Presigned URL to download the output file.

source.description: Relative path of the output file within the output folder.

CustomScriptJobSucceededResponse.title: Execution Success
CustomScriptJobSucceededResponse.description: Returned when the Custom Scripts job completes successfully.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the status was recorded.

outputs.description: Presigned download URLs for each output file.

CustomScriptJobRunningResponse.title: Execution Running
CustomScriptJobRunningResponse.description: Returned while the Custom Scripts job is still executing. Poll again after a short interval.

CustomScriptJobFailedResponse.title: Execution Failed
CustomScriptJobFailedResponse.description: Returned when the Custom Scripts job has failed.

errors.description: List of errors describing what went wrong.

CustomScriptJobNotFoundResponse.description: Returned when the job ID does not exist.
